title: Prompt Weighting and Emphasis
concept: Some systems let users increase or decrease a specific word's influence, giving finer control over which prompt elements dominate the output.

exercises.prompt: When you say a word LOUDER in a sentence, what usually happens?

options.text: People pay more attention to that word

exercises.explain: Exactly! Saying something louder (or with more emphasis) makes it stand out more. AI prompts can work the same way with special weighting tricks.

cards.title: Turn the Volume Up on a Word
cards.body: What if you could make one word in your prompt 'louder' than the rest — so the AI pays extra attention to it? That's exactly what prompt weighting lets you do!

cards.title: What Is Prompt Weighting?
cards.body: Prompt weighting means giving certain words in your prompt more or less importance. Some AI tools let you 'boost' a word (like making it stronger) or 'lower' a word (making it less important), so you get more control over the final result.

cards.title: Like a Sound Mixer
cards.body: Think of a music mixer with sliders for drums, guitar, and vocals. Turning the vocal slider up makes the singer stand out more. Prompt weighting works the same way — you turn certain words 'up' or 'down' so they stand out more or less in the AI's output.

cards.title: Boosting a Detail
cards.body: Imagine prompting an image AI with: 'a (giant) dragon in a small village.' By weighting 'giant' higher, the AI makes the dragon extra huge and dominant in the picture, instead of just an average-sized dragon.

cards.title: Myth: All Words Matter Equally
cards.body: Myth: Every word in a prompt is treated the same by AI. Truth: Some systems let you adjust how much 'weight' or focus certain words get, meaning you can push some ideas to the front and shrink others.

cards.title: Same Words, Different Emphasis
cards.body: The words 'red car' and 'small dog' appear in both prompts below, but weighting changes what the AI focuses on most.

left.text: A big bold red car takes over the image, with a small dog barely visible in the background.

right.text: A cute small dog is front and center, while the red car appears tiny and less noticeable.

exercises.prompt: What does prompt weighting let you do?

options.text: Delete words from the AI's memory forever
Increase or decrease how much influence a word has

exercises.explain: Prompt weighting is about adjusting influence — making a word matter more or less in the final output, not about speed or memory.

exercises.prompt: Prompt weighting means every word in a prompt is always treated with equal importance.
exercises.explain: False! Prompt weighting is the opposite — it lets certain words matter more or less than others.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea.
exercises.sentence: Prompt weighting lets you give a word more or less ___ in the AI's output.
exercises.explain: Weighting changes how much influence, or importance, a word has on the final result.

exercises.prompt: Which prompt shows an example of prompt weighting being used?
exercises.explain: The '++' symbol after 'sparkling' shows the word is being boosted or weighted higher, which is a sign of prompt weighting.

exercises.prompt: You want an AI image generator to create a picture where a tiny kitten is the main focus, much more important than a large sofa in the background. What should you do using prompt weighting?

options.text: Increase the weight on 'kitten' so it becomes the dominant focus
Increase the weight on 'sofa' so it becomes the dominant focus
Remove all weighting since it won't change anything
Type the prompt in all capital letters instead

exercises.explain: To make the kitten the main focus, you should boost the weight on 'kitten' so the AI emphasizes it more than the sofa in the final image.
